images.caption: Figure 1:Left: TRD refines student-generated trajectoriesyoy_{o}into improved trajectoriesyry_{r}, which are then used for distillation.Right: Avg@16 performance comparison between OPD/OPSD and TRD across all evaluation tasks under different base models.

Figure 2:Left and Middle:Under prefix failure, the teacher distribution becomes a mixture with two modes. By their respective mode-covering and mode-seeking properties, forward KL is dominated by the correction-onset region, while reverse KL is dominated by the wrong-continuation region.Right:Supervision signal provided by the teacher under prefix failure.

Figure 3:Empirical observations of prefix failure under standard OPSD (yoy_{o}, forward and reverse KL).Left: Per-token KL by correct/incorrect rollouts.Middle: Teacher-student perplexity gap.Right: Teacher’s epistemic-token mass.

Figure 4:Trajectory analysis.Left: OPSD training-corpus trajectory length on Qwen3-8B, with the orange line showing verifier accuracy.Middle: OPD AMOBench correct-rollout length distribution.Right: OPD Pass@kkfrom theK=128K{=}128AMOBench rollouts, with thek=1k{=}1point equal to Avg@128.

images.caption: Figure 5:Training-trajectory analysis on Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507.Top row: with-CoT subset (n=4,419n{=}4{,}419).Bottom row: Without-CoT subset (n=35,826n{=}35{,}826).Left: Length distribution ofy⋆y^{\star},yoy_{o},yry_{r}, Withyoy_{o}andyry_{r}split into correct (left) and incorrect (right) halves.Middle: Verifier accuracy of the three trajectories.Right: Joint outcome ofyoy_{o}andyry_{r}(2×22\!\times\!2confusion); fail→\topass cells reflect prefix-failure recovery, pass→\tofail cells quantify the price.

Figure 6:Training-trajectory analysis on Qwen3-8B.Top row: with-CoT subset (n=4,419n{=}4{,}419).Bottom row: Without-CoT subset (n=35,826n{=}35{,}826).Left: Length distribution ofy⋆y^{\star},yoy_{o},yry_{r}, withyoy_{o}andyry_{r}split into correct (left) and incorrect (right) halves.Middle: Verifier accuracy of the three trajectories.Right: Joint outcome ofyoy_{o}andyry_{r}(2×22\!\times\!2confusion); fail→\topass cells reflect prefix-failure recovery, pass→\tofail cells quantify the price.

Figure 7:Test-rollout analysis on AMOBench (Qwen3-8B,K=16K{=}16samples per question).Left: Per-rollout response-length distribution split by correctness (correct on the left half, incorrect on the right half of each violin).Middle / Right: Avg@16 and Pass@16 stratified by base-model difficulty bucket, where B0groups the2323questions the base model fails on all1616attempts, B11–88the1212questions solved between11and88times, and B99–1616the44questions solved at least99times.
